channel.title: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: Backroom Deals Behind Secretary-General Candidate Races
item.description: Soon, the United Nations Security Council will begin meeting with candidates vying to be the next UN secretary-general. The world will follow along as the 15-member Council starts holding private consultations in the coming days, releases occasional media statements and votes in secret straw polls to express their views on the nominees. But another, even ... Read more

item.title: Security Council LIVE: Open debate on children and armed conflict
item.description: The UN Security Council is meeting on Thursday for an open debate on strengthening protections for children caught in armed conflict. The Secretary-General's latest report reveals that in 2025, soldiers and Government forces were responsible for more grave violations against children in armed conflict than non-State armed groups, marking a first in 30 years of UN monitoring.  The report verified 38,558 grave violations such as killing, recruitment and abduction, affecting 24,174 children, many of whom suffered multiple violations.

item.title: Stranded Hormuz seafarers begin mass evacuation operation
item.description: As the UN International Maritime Organization (IMO) released more details of its plan to evacuate more than 11,000 seafarers stranded in the Strait of Hormuz, one mariner caught up in the emergency has described the ever-present fear of coming under attack.

item.title: New global rules clear the road for driverless vehicles
item.description: A UN vehicle standards forum has approved the first global regulations for fully autonomous driving systems (ADS), marking a major step towards the safe deployment of self-driving vehicles.

item.title: UN chief: Targeting methane is a climate battle ‘we can win’
item.description: Amid efforts to cool global warming, the battle hinges on targeting such super-pollutants as methane, which emits one third of the world’s greenhouse gases and “is a fight we can win”, the UN chief on Wednesday.

item.title: Lives at risk in DR Congo as Ebola outbreak continues to outpace response
item.description: The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) continues to spread faster than aid efforts can keep pace, despite significant gains in treatment capacity and growing community engagement, the World Health Organization (WHO) warned on Wednesday.

item.title: How the stories we tell about ageing shape our future
item.description: The way societies talk about ageing can shape everything from public policy to people's own expectations of later life, the United Nations Economic Commission for Europe (UNECE) said on Wednesday in a new report that calls  for shifting away from narratives that portray older persons as a burden.

item.title: World News in Brief: Students injured in Sudan drone strike, dangerous returns in south Lebanon, celebrating women diplomats
item.description: Ongoing fighting in the Sudanese city of El Obeid continues to endanger civilians and damage infrastructure, the United Nations said on Wednesday.

item.title: Young peacebuilders: Funding matters, but so do trust and partnerships
item.description: Young people working on promoting peace in some of the world’s most fragile and polarized places say financial support remains essential, but money alone will not sustain their efforts to organize youth to participate in peacebuilding.

item.title: New GEF Project Raises Hope for Change in India’s Indigenous Lake Community
item.description: At dawn, when the waters of Dumboor Lake lie still under a pale grey sky, Santo Chakma, 63, nudges his narrow wooden boat into a reservoir that swallowed his childhood. The lake is a growing attraction for tourists who come here in search of beauty and tranquillity, with dozens of islands scattered across a vast […]

item.title: WORLD CUP: ‘FIFA Has Placed Itself on the Side of the Polluters, Not the Rest of the Planet’
item.description: CIVICUS speaks about the climate impacts of the 2026 World Cup with Frank Huisingh, founder of Fossil Free Football, a fan-led group that campaigns to end fossil fuel sponsorship in football and make the game more sustainable. The 2026 World Cup is the biggest in the tournament’s history, and the most polluting. With 48 […]

item.title: Of 40 Million People Living with HIV today, 32.1 Million are now on Treatment, Living Long & Healthy
item.description: Remarks by Winnie Byanyima, Executive Director of the Joint UN Programme on HIV/AIDS (UNAIDS), at a High-level Meeting in the General Assembly Hall, 22 June 2026
